title: Zereshk Polo ba Morgh (Barberry Rice with Chicken)
description: A classic Persian dish featuring saffron-infused chicken served alongside fluffy basmati rice studded with tart barberries and pistachios.

ingredients.note: Ground, bloomed in 2 tbsp hot water

instructions.text: In a large pot, heat 2 tbsp of vegetable oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until golden brown (about 8-10 minutes).
Add minced garlic, turmeric, salt, and black pepper. Stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the chicken pieces to the pot and sear on all sides until lightly browned.

Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes to remove its raw flavor. Add 1.5 cups of water and half of the bloomed saffron. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 45 minutes until chicken is tender.

While the chicken cooks, prepare the rice. Boil 6 cups of water with 2 tbsp salt. Add soaked basmati rice and boil for 5-7 minutes until the outside is soft but the center is still slightly firm. Drain.

In a non-stick pot, melt 1 tbsp oil. Add a layer of rice, shaping it into a pyramid. Poke holes in the rice with a spoon handle. Pour 1/4 cup of water mixed with 1 tbsp oil over the rice. Cover with a lid wrapped in a clean towel and steam on low heat for 45 minutes.
In a small pan, melt butter over low heat. Add washed barberries and sugar. Sauté gently for 1-2 minutes until they plump up (do not burn). Turn off the heat.
Mix 1 cup of the steamed rice with the remaining bloomed saffron and the barberries.
To serve, spread the white rice on a platter, top with the saffron-barberry rice mix, and sprinkle with slivered pistachios. Serve the chicken in a separate bowl with its sauce.

tips: Wash the barberries carefully as they can contain grit. Never fry them on high heat, as they will burn and turn bitter immediately.
storage_notes: Store the chicken and rice in separate airtight containers in the fridge for up to 3 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop or microwave.
serve_with: Mast-o-Khiar (cucumber yogurt), Salad Shirazi, and Sabzi Khordan (fresh herb platter).

substitutions.note: Use less sugar if substituting with sweetened dried cranberries.
For a vegetarian version, simmer tofu or chickpeas in the tomato saffron sauce.
